name: agent-skills
description: 18 slash-command skills that fix specific agent failure modes
author: agent-skills contributors

skills.name: setup
skills.description: Run once per repo. Creates .agent-context/ for shared project knowledge.

skills.name: route-or-answer
skills.description: Decide: answer directly or call a tool. Stops unnecessary API calls.

skills.name: clarify-intent
skills.description: Ask one targeted question when intent is ambiguous. Nothing when it's clear.

skills.name: simplify-scope
skills.description: Cut task to minimum that satisfies the goal. Stops scope creep.

skills.name: assumption-check
skills.description: List hidden assumptions before executing. Block dangerous ones.

skills.name: compress-context
skills.description: Shrink accumulated context 60-75% without losing decisions.

skills.name: cache-hint
skills.description: Tag output parts as reusable vs must-recompute.

skills.name: caveman-summary
skills.description: Shortest possible version of any text. Stop writing when done.

skills.name: detect-contradiction
skills.description: Compare draft against source data. Catch factual conflicts before sending.

skills.name: validate-output
skills.description: Confirm output answers what was asked in the format requested.

skills.name: prevent-redundancy
skills.description: Remove content already said earlier in the conversation.

skills.name: format-response
skills.description: Apply correct formatting for Slack / terminal / email / JSON API.

skills.name: tool-call-guard
skills.description: Validate tool params before execution. Block bad calls.

skills.name: error-to-action
skills.description: Convert raw error strings to plain-English explanation + next action.

skills.name: tone-match
skills.description: Detect user emotional register. Adjust response tone before replying.

skills.name: explain-depth
skills.description: Calibrate explanation depth to actual expertise. No over-explaining.

skills.name: humanize-output
skills.description: Strip hollow AI-isms. Great question!, Certainly!, Absolutely! — all gone.
